TF-E5.Summary: MVP 5: Identity & Credentials — JIT, Delegation, Consent, NHI, Local LLM
TF-E5.Description:
Implement identity and credential management: DelegationContext framework, JIT Credential Broker, Agentic Consent flows, NHI X.509 registry, confused deputy prevention, and Local LLM fallback for privacy-sensitive AI requests.

SCOPE:
- DelegationContext propagated through API → orchestrator → agents → MCP
- CredentialBroker: short-lived tokens TTL ≤900s
- Agentic consent modal enforcement on AI endpoints
- NHI registry with X.509 certificates for agent identity
- Local LLM fallback per docs/LOCAL-LLM.md
- Confused deputy prevention: agents never use own credentials

EFFORT: L (40 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Security, Backend, Frontend
DEPENDENCIES: TF-E4 complete — security layer operational

EPIC LINK: epic/taskflow-implementation
FEATURE BRANCH: epic/TF-E5-identity-credentials

REFERENCE DOCS:
- docs/TaskFlowAI_Project_Proposal.md (§ Identity & Credential Management)
- docs/IDENTITY-PROPAGATION.md (when created)
- docs/AGENTIC-CONSENT.md (when created)
- docs/LOCAL-LLM.md (when created)

SUCCESS CRITERIA:
- All MCP calls use delegation token scoped to user intent
- AI endpoints return 403 without valid consent record
- CredentialBroker issues token with expires_at within 15 min
- NHI certificates registered for all 6 agents
- PII-classified request routes to local LLM when enabled

Implement DelegationContext dataclass and propagation through request → graph → MCP chain.

USE CASES:
- Orchestrator creates delegation context per agent with intent read_tasks
- MCP client presents delegation token to Credential Broker
- Expired delegation token rejected before MCP call

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Create backend/security/delegation.py: DelegationContext dataclass, create_delegation(), validate_delegation()
- Fields: user_id, session_id, agent_id, intent, permissions, issued_at, expires_at, parent_trace_id
- Expand backend/kernel/identity_manager.py to issue and validate contexts
- Middleware: attach delegation to graph state from JWT user
- Alembic: delegation_audit table optional for issued tokens log
- Document in docs/IDENTITY-PROPAGATION.md

EFFORT: M (6 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend, Security
DEPENDENCIES: TF-022, TF-006

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Valid context passes validation
- Expired context raises DelegationExpiredError
- intent mismatch with MCP tool raises ConfusedDeputyError
- trace_id propagated to parent_trace_id

EDGE CASES:
- TTL >900s request: clamp to 900
- Missing permission in context: deny before MCP
- Clock skew 30s: grace window configurable
- Revoked session: delegation invalid immediately

Implement CredentialBroker for Just-In-Time short-lived credential issuance to MCP clients.

USE CASES:
- Context Agent requests supabase read_tasks credential valid 15 minutes
- Broker validates consent before issuing OAuth/access token
- Credential issuance logged in sealed audit trail

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Create backend/security/vault.py: Credential, CredentialBroker classes
- get_credential(user_id, service, intent, ttl_seconds=900) -> Credential
- Services: supabase; intents: read_tasks, read_projects, update_tasks
- Dev: in-memory TTL cache; prod interface for Redis/Vault
- Log via audit_seal.py: credential_issued event
- credential_issuance_total{service,intent} metric
- Settings: VAULT_MODE=env|memory, no hardcoded secrets

EFFORT: L (8 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend, Security
DEPENDENCIES: TF-049, TF-046

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Issued credential expires within TTL
- Missing consent raises CredentialDeniedError
- Cache hit within TTL doesn't double-audit
- MCP client uses broker not raw DATABASE_URL in agent path

EDGE CASES:
- Token exchange failure: no partial credential returned
- TTL=0: ValueError
- Broker unavailable: fail closed for MCP, degraded AI response
- user_id consent mismatch: deny

Implement time-bounded AI consent records and enforcement on all AI API endpoints.

USE CASES:
- User accepts AI consent modal → consent record stored with 30-day TTL
- User invokes AI without consent → 403 consent_required
- Consent expires → user prompted to re-authorize
- User revokes consent → AI endpoints blocked immediately

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Alembic migration 007_consent_records: user_id, workspace_id, scope, granted_at, expires_at, revoked_at
- Create backend/services/consent_service.py: grant, revoke, check_consent, is_valid
- Enforce in backend/api/v1/ai.py before graph invoke
- Wire frontend ConsentPromptModal to POST /api/v1/consent/ai
- Transaction-aware: consent scoped to workspace_id
- Document in docs/AGENTIC-CONSENT.md

EFFORT: M (6 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend, Frontend
DEPENDENCIES: TF-040, TF-037

TESTING CRITERIA:
- AI call without consent returns 403 with consent_required body
- Grant consent allows AI call
- Revoked consent blocks immediately
- Expired consent requires re-grant

EDGE CASES:
- Consent grant while revoked: new record supersedes
- Admin cannot grant consent for another user
- Consent scope minimal: read_tasks, read_projects only
- GDPR delete user: cascade consent records

Register Non-Human Identities for all agents with X.509 certificates and weekly rotation stub.

USE CASES:
- Each agent has certificate CN=context-agent with permissions=tasks:read
- Kernel validates agent certificate before MCP invocation
- Compromised agent cert revocable via CRL stub

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Create backend/security/nhi_registry.py: AgentIdentity, NHIRegistry
- Certificate fields: agent_id, canonical_role, permissions, issued_at, expires_at
- Dev: self-signed certs generated at startup; prod: Vault PKI interface stub
- Register all 6 agents on startup if not present
- Private keys in Vault/env, never in repo
- Document blast-radius per agent in registry metadata

EFFORT: M (6 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend, Security
DEPENDENCIES: TF-049

TESTING CRITERIA:
- All 6 agents registered in NHIRegistry
- Invalid cert rejected by kernel identity check
- Certificate expiry within 7 days triggers renewal warning log
- Unit tests >=8 scenarios

EDGE CASES:
- Registry init failure: block agent MCP calls (fail secure)
- Duplicate agent_id: reject registration
- Permissions exceed allowlist: reject cert creation
- mTLS future: document extension point in AGENT.md

Wire Local LLM (Llama 3.1) fallback for privacy-sensitive and PII-classified AI requests.

USE CASES:
- Task description classified confidential_pii routes to local Ollama instance
- Primary cloud LLM rate limited → fallback to local if LOCAL_LLM_ENABLED=true
- Offline dev environment runs AI features without cloud API keys

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Create docs/LOCAL-LLM.md: hardware reqs, benchmarks, privacy rules
- Expand backend/llm/router.py: LocalLLMProvider (Ollama API)
- Settings: LOCAL_LLM_ENABLED, LOCAL_LLM_BASE_URL, LOCAL_LLM_MODEL
- Classification hook in planner: data_classification metadata → force_local
- Fallback order: primary → gpt-4o-mini → local

EFFORT: M (6 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend
DEPENDENCIES: TF-025

TESTING CRITERIA:
- force_local=true calls Ollama mock endpoint
- LOCAL_LLM_ENABLED=false skips local fallback
- PII classification triggers local in integration test
- Latency logged separately for local provider

EDGE CASES:
- Ollama unavailable: raise LLMError with clear message
- Local model context window smaller: truncate context with warning
- Mixed cloud/local in same graph: per-agent provider selection
- No GPU: document CPU inference latency expectations

TF-E5.Summary: MVP 5 Identity Integration Tests and Docs
TF-E5.Description:
End-to-end tests for delegation, consent, broker, and confused deputy prevention.

USE CASES:
- CI validates full identity chain: JWT → delegation → broker → MCP
- test_confused_deputy.py ensures agent cannot escalate permissions
- docs updated for identity epic completion

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Create backend/tests/security/test_delegation.py, test_confused_deputy.py, test_consent.py, test_vault.py
- Integration: AI request with consent → delegation → MCP mock
- Update docs/IDENTITY-PROPAGATION.md, docs/AGENTIC-CONSENT.md, docs/PLAN.md
- proof/mvp5/ artifacts

EFFORT: M (4 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Testing, Documentation
DEPENDENCIES: TF-049, TF-050, TF-051, TF-052, TF-053

TESTING CRITERIA:
- All identity security tests pass
- Confused deputy attack scenario blocked in test
- Documentation complete for MVP 5

EDGE CASES:
- Mock Vault in CI without real secrets
- Consent race: two parallel grants idempotent
- Integration test timeout 60s
